title: thymus lanuginosus var. pannonicus
intro: Thymus lanuginosus var. pannonicus, also known as Hungarian thyme, is a subspecies of the thymus lanuginosus plant, a perennial herb belonging to the mint family. It is native to Central and Eastern Europe, with medicinal properties attributed to its essential oils, particularly thymol, which has antibacterial and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion, due to its carminative and expectorant properties. Thymus lanuginosus var. pannonicus can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues.
